human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for venue-specific execution boundaries, source quality, ticketing and accessibility handling, fraud and screening escalation, scenario coverage, commercialization notes, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to venue execution work for event readiness, ticketing, accessible seating, ingress, guest-impact handling, and closeout evidence and does not claim pricing, refund, fraud, legal, or security-command authority.

Authority sources cover DOJ accessibility guidance, FTC fee-transparency and BOTS Act guidance, CFPB billing-error guidance, CISA venue-security guidance, and IAVM venue-operation frameworks, so workflow, terminology, evidence, and failure handling are materially changed by the named industry.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete prerequisites, missing completion evidence, payment mismatches, refund exceptions, duplicated-ticket and screening escalations, high-volume ingress, conflicting-policy handling, adjacent-lane rejection, low-confidence escalation, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/arts-entertainment-and-recreation/venue-operations-specialist/ so venue operations work remains separated from guest-services policy ownership, pricing and revenue authority, fraud investigation, and security command.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around refund overrides, pricing changes, accessibility conflicts, suspicious tickets, and safety-screening escalation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated venue operations specialist that supports evidence-backed ticketing and event execution, exception handling, and completion control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
